_comment: Medical timeline events for Kevin Lane. Add new events in chronological order. eventType: 'visit' | 'lab' | 'procedure' | 'hospitalization' | 'legal' | 'communication' | 'milestone'. legalSignificance: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | null.

events.title: New Patient — Flu/Body Aches
events.summary: Flu-like symptoms, body aches, fatigue, urinary frequency. No CK or metabolic evaluation.

events.title: Kidney Stone Symptoms
events.summary: Severe left side pain, frequent urination. Microscopic blood in urine. Kidney stone protocol initiated.

events.title: Headaches/Migraines — Elevated BP
events.summary: Headaches/migraines worsening ×2 months. Elevated BP 100 mg TABS ordered.

events.title: Back Pain / Scoliosis Imaging
events.summary: Lower/upper back pain daily since 3/2012. Elevated BP. MRI ordered. X-ray: mild levoscoliosis, Cobb angle 12°.

events.title: Labs — High LDL, Low Albumin
events.summary: CBC, CMP, lipid, A1C ordered. High LDL (121), low albumin (0.20), high globulin (6.8), low A/G ratio (0.0). No CK ordered.

events.title: Annual Physical — No CK Ordered
events.summary: 43 yo male. Low back pain, anxiety, depression, ADHD. HLD elevated. Mentioned rhabdomyolysis history in notes. No CK ordered. No metabolic evaluation.
events.details: Patient had exercise-related myalgias. Dr. Kayani documented 'low back pain stable' without ordering CK or metabolic workup despite this being the standard of care for patients with prior rhabdo history.

events.title: 44 Labs Ordered — CK Omitted
events.summary: Comprehensive lab panel ordered. CK intentionally or negligently omitted despite patient's symptom history. Patient hospitalized 6 days later with CK 7,696 U/L.
events.details: Standard of care required CK monitoring. Dr. Kayani ordered 44 labs but not CK. Six days later, patient presented to ER with first near-fatal rhabdomyolysis episode.

events.title: FIRST RHABDOMYOLYSIS — Hospitalized
events.summary: Acute non-traumatic rhabdomyolysis. CK >10,000 U/L on admission. 23-hour observation minimum. CK peak 7,696+ U/L.
events.details: Exercise recovery challenges with intense workout sessions noted in records. First near-fatal episode.

events.title: Hospital Discharge — First Rhabdo
events.summary: Discharged with advice: avoid strenuous activity, CK recheck in 1 week, aggressive hydration. No specialist referral for underlying metabolic cause.

events.title: Post-Rhabdo Follow-up — No Referral
events.summary: Patient reported rhabdomyolysis episode (Jan 25-26). CK normalized. Dr. Kayani acknowledged episode — NO preventive counseling or referral. NEVER disclosed to pain management.
events.details: This is the critical breach. Dr. Kayani knew of the Jan 2024 rhabdo but never communicated it to Dr. Kamath (pain management), who later performed the RFA procedure that triggered the second catastrophic event.

events.title: Labs — Normal CK 95 U/L
events.summary: CK 95 (normal). Comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, lipids, PSA, urinalysis. No acute concerns noted. No metabolic myopathy investigation initiated.
events.details: CK was within normal limits but patient was between episodes. No metabolic evaluation ordered despite prior rhabdo history.

events.title: Annual Physical — Rhabdo History Mentioned
events.summary: Dr. Kayani noted rhabdomyolysis history in chart. Elevated LDL 125, high Vitamin B12 (1113 pg/mL). No CK monitoring plan established.

events.title: Pain Mgmt New Patient — Dr. Kamath
events.summary: Initial pain evaluation (9/10 pain). Scheduled MRIs, initiated Lidocaine patch trial. No rhabdo history in referral from Dr. Kayani.
events.details: Dr. Kamath began treating without knowledge of rhabdomyolysis history — a direct result of Dr. Kayani's failure to disclose.

events.title: Spine MRIs (Cervical & Lumbar)
events.summary: MRI confirmed degenerative changes: disc bulge L5-S1, new mild left neural foraminal stenosis at C3-4. Baseline for pain management.

events.title: Bilateral Facet Medial Branch Nerve Block #1
events.summary: Bilateral L3-4, L4-5, L5-S1. 90% relief for 24 hours. No CK check. No rhabdo precautions.

events.title: Bilateral Facet Medial Branch Nerve Block #2
events.summary: >80% relief reported. Confirmed candidacy for RFA. No metabolic precautions.

events.title: RADIOFREQUENCY ABLATION (RFA) — Critical Negligence Trigger
events.summary: Bilateral RFA L3-4, L4-5, L5-S1 facet medial branch nerves. 95% relief reported. NO metabolic precautions. NO CK monitoring. Dr. Kamath had NO knowledge of Jan 2024 rhabdo.
events.details: RFA is a known high-risk intervention in patients with metabolic/mitochondrial disorders. Pre-procedure CK screening and metabolic stabilization are required per published literature. Dr. Kayani's failure to disclose the rhabdo history directly caused this procedure to be performed without necessary precautions.

events.title: 82 Labs Ordered — CK Omitted Again
events.summary: 82 labs collected — CK again omitted. Patient hospitalized 7 days later with CK >22,052 U/L.
events.details: Second instance of CK omission following a prior rhabdo episode. Patient was hospitalized exactly one week later with the catastrophic second event.

events.title: FPLOL Physical — Imminent Crisis
events.summary: FPLOL follow-up during which CK was noted at >22,052 U/L (critical). Patient rushed to hospital.

events.title: SECOND CATASTROPHIC RHABDOMYOLYSIS
events.summary: CK >22,000 U/L. Aldolase 153. AST/ALT elevated. UA 1+ blood → myoglobinuria. Emergency hospitalization. Permanent muscle and cellular injury.
events.details: This is the primary injury date (SOL runs from Aug 6, 2025). Directly caused by: (1) Dr. Kayani's failure to disclose prior rhabdo to Dr. Kamath, enabling the RFA procedure; (2) ongoing failure to monitor CK.

events.title: Post-Hospital Follow-up
events.summary: Post-hospital follow-up. Wife present as witness. Rheumatology referral documented. Continued failure to address underlying metabolic cause.

events.title: Pain Mgmt — 'No Relation to Metabolic Myopathy'
events.summary: Dr. Kamath stated 'no relation to metabolic myopathy.' Patient worsening diffuse pain. Prescribed tramadol, lidocaine. Referred for medical marijuana.

events.title: Rheumatology — CK 92, Documented Recovery
events.summary: Post-rhabdo autoimmune workup. CK 92 U/L (recovery). Negative MSA Panel. Recommended avoiding all exercise.

events.title: Neurology — Invitae Panel Ordered
events.summary: Dr. Rosario suspected metabolic/mitochondrial myopathy. Ordered Invitae Metabolic Myopathy Panel — stated it was 'critical for diagnosis and management.'
events.details: This is the start of the 48-day PA delay by Dr. Rosario's office (Sep 9 – Oct 28, 2025).

events.title: PA Submission Implied But Delayed
events.summary: Office faxed order to Invitae. PA submission implied delayed — office told patient 'insurance information faxed.' Kit mailed but PA not submitted.

events.title: Reduction-in-Force (RIF) Notice Issued
events.summary: RIF notice issued to Kevin Lane. This is the critical employment event that Dr. Rosario's delay prevented adequate response to — no formal medical documentation or accommodations possible.

events.title: PA Finally Submitted — 48 Days Late
events.summary: Prior authorization submitted 48 days after order. Patient was under active RIF notice and had been denied timely diagnosis. Violates Fla. Stat. §627.42392.

events.title: New PCP — Comprehensive Workup
events.summary: New patient consultation. Immediate comprehensive labs and referrals to genetics, neurology, CT scan. Scheduled due to changing doctors after abandonment.

events.title: Genetic Sample Sent via FedEx
events.summary: Patient sent sample via FedEx — 'Cannot wait for prior authorization any longer — too urgent.'

events.title: Neurology — Probable Mitochondrial Myopathy
events.summary: Confirmed probable metabolic/mitochondrial myopathy. Ordered MRI Brain WO Contrast, MR Angio Brain WO Contrast, and nerve conduction study.

events.title: INVITAE RESULTS — Mitochondrial Dysfunction Confirmed
events.summary: RQ7553840: Two rare VUS in AGK gene (mitochondrial lipid metabolism) + truncating VUS in AMPD1 gene (~94% loss of enzyme function). Scientific proof of underlying metabolic dysfunction.
events.details: This confirms the vulnerability that Dr. Kayani failed to screen for. AGK variants affect mitochondrial lipid metabolism; AMPD1 truncating mutation causes ~94% loss of enzyme function — directly explaining recurrent rhabdomyolysis.

events.title: EMG/NCS — Normal, Confirms Metabolic Cause
events.summary: EMG/Nerve Conduction Study entirely normal. No inflammatory myopathy, mononeuropathy, or myotonic discharges. Definitively rules out structural cause — permanent functional impairment is metabolic.

events.title: MRI Brain & MR Angio — Normal
events.summary: MRI Brain WO IV Contrast and MR Angio Head WO Contrast — both normal. Definitively rules out structural or neurological causes for symptoms.

events.title: Medical Malpractice Intake Consultation Prepared
events.summary: Attorney-ready intake consultation prepared documenting dual-defendant case. Economic damages range: $650,000–$1,300,000.

events.title: Pain Mgmt Appointment Cancelled — Abandonment
events.summary: Dr. Kamath's office cancelled final pain management follow-up. Will cancel due to abandonment of care — documented in chart.

events.title: Mayo Clinic Genetics Consultation Scheduled
events.summary: Consultation with Clinical Biochemical Geneticist for advanced testing and variant correlation. Necessary to move VUS findings to definitive genetic etiology — final medical support for permanent disability.
